workLog.detail: Issue #2629: tests/e2e/test_cli_hook_e2e.py launches copilot/claude via subprocess.run([name, ...]); on windows-latest CreateProcess does not apply PATHEXT, npm shim is copilot.cmd, raises FileNotFoundError WinError 2. Confirmed in real files: 4 bare-name launches (lines ~232 install, ~247 run, ~270 uninstall, ~312 claude).
Wrote tests/test_cli_exec.py against scripts.cli_exec.resolve_executable (not yet created). Ran: uv run pytest tests/test_cli_exec.py -q

scripts/cli_exec.py: resolve_executable scans PATH applying PATHEXT (Windows ';' sep, case-insensitive os.listdir match, no pathlib so WindowsPath is not instantiated on POSIX); POSIX returns bare name; raises FileNotFoundError when unresolved. Ran: uv run pytest tests/test_cli_exec.py -q
3 mutations of scripts/cli_exec.py: (1) always return name -> 4 failed; (2) case-sensitive entry==target -> 3 failed; (3) split on os.pathsep -> 3 failed. Restored from backup; final run passed.
workLog.result: passed: each mutation produced failures, restored module 6 passed

workLog.detail: tests/e2e/test_cli_hook_e2e.py: routed all 4 CLI launches through resolve_executable('copilot'/'claude'). Added regression guard test_e2e_launchers_route_through_resolver reading the e2e source. Mutated e2e back to bare name -> guard failed; restored -> guard passed. Ran: uv run pytest tests/test_cli_exec.py tests/e2e/ -q
workLog.result: passed: 11 passed, gated e2e deselected by design

workLog.detail: uvx ruff check + ruff format on scripts/cli_exec.py, tests/test_cli_exec.py, tests/e2e/test_cli_hook_e2e.py
workLog.result: passed: All checks passed; files formatted

workLog.step: act note (windows-latest cannot run under act)
workLog.detail: act is Linux-only; nightly-cli-smoke.yml smoke job runs on windows-latest and needs live secrets (SMOKE_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, SMOKE_COPILOT_CLI_TOKEN). gh act cannot execute the windows e2e. Verification is the new unit test that simulates the Windows PATHEXT layout on Linux (the Linux-exercisable substitute the issue asks for). No workflow YAML changed.
workLog.result: act not applicable for windows job; verified via unit test instead
